us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: ICE suspends most vehicle stops after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine
bullets: The Trump administration ordered ICE to suspend most vehicle stops during enforcement operations.
The move followed fatal shootings involving ICE officers in Texas and Maine.
DHS also said ICE arrest teams will include at least one agent with a body camera.

articles.title: Senate Democrats block $1 trillion-plus defense bill over Iran war objections
bullets: Senate Democrats refused to advance the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday.
They cited opposition to President Trump’s handling of the Iran war.
The package included higher Pentagon spending and a military pay raise.

articles.title: Cyclospora illnesses surge as officials investigate possible food source
bullets: AP reported a record surge in U.S. cyclospora infections in 2026.
Michigan officials are examining lettuce as a possible source.
The parasite can cause prolonged diarrhea and other gastrointestinal symptoms.

articles.title: Supreme Court justices make rare congressional appearance on court security
bullets: AP reported that justices testified before Congress about Supreme Court security.
The appearance came amid broader political and institutional tensions in Washington.
The hearing was part of a wider day of federal government developments.

articles.title: Report links Trump clean-energy cuts to $83 billion in delayed or canceled projects
bullets: Reuters reported that scaled-back federal clean-energy support has affected hundreds of projects.
A BlueGreen Alliance report put delayed or canceled investment at $83 billion.
The findings were released Tuesday amid debate over U.S. energy policy.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Iran threatens more energy export routes after U.S. renews blockade
bullets: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard threatened to close additional export corridors benefiting the U.S. and allies.
The warning followed Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz and a renewed U.S. naval blockade.
Analysts warned of risks to the Red Sea’s Bab el-Mandeb route through Houthi action.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill about a dozen in Gaza despite months-old ceasefire
bullets: AP reported Israeli airstrikes killed about a dozen people in Gaza over two days.
Local health officials said the strikes were part of a wider campaign.
The attacks occurred despite a months-old ceasefire with Hamas.

articles.title: UAE says Iranian missiles hit tankers in Hormuz, killing an Indian crew member
bullets: Reuters reported two Emirati oil tankers were struck in the Strait of Hormuz.
The UAE said one Indian sailor was killed and eight others were wounded.
The incident marked another escalation in the strategic waterway.

articles.title: U.S. senators rally behind revised Russia sanctions bill
bullets: A bipartisan group of senators advanced a revised Russia sanctions effort.
The bill would target Russian officials, financial institutions and energy projects.
Sponsors said China and India are among the top Russian energy buyers affected by tariff pressure.

articles.title: Asian markets rise after softer U.S. inflation data
bullets: Reuters reported Asian markets were higher Wednesday after U.S. inflation slowed unexpectedly.
Oil eased after the U.S. scrapped a proposed Hormuz shipping levy.
Investors also weighed strong Wall Street bank earnings and IBM’s steep share drop.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: India moves to account for nationals after sailor killed in Hormuz attack
bullets: Hindustan Times reported a government push to account for every Indian in the affected maritime zone.
The move followed the death of an Indian sailor in attacks on tankers near Hormuz.
The crisis has heightened concern for Indian seafarers working Gulf routes.

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike enters day 18 as health concerns grow
bullets: Hindustan Times reported Wangchuk’s hunger strike entered its 18th day.
A Delhi High Court plea sought intervention to end the fast.
Supporters said a broader hunger strike was planned.

articles.title: Four arrested in Manipur Assam Rifles convoy ambush case
bullets: Hindustan Times reported four arrests connected to an Assam Rifles convoy ambush in Manipur.
Security agencies are probing possible insurgent links.
The case adds to continuing security concerns in the region.

articles.title: India–UK trade agreement takes effect with zero-duty access for key exports
bullets: NDTV reported the India–UK trade agreement came into force on July 15.
Textiles, leather, footwear, gems, jewellery and plastics exports get zero-duty access to Britain.
The deal is expected to benefit several Indian export sectors.

articles.title: Centre bars imports of goods made with forced labour amid tariff pressure
bullets: The Indian Express reported the Centre barred imports of goods using forced labour.
The move came amid a new U.S. tariff plan affecting India.
Officials said authorities can act suo motu without waiting for a complaint.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top U.S. market movers from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: IBM plunges 25% after weak preliminary outlook
bullets: IBM was the worst Dow performer, falling 25.21% on Tuesday.
The company warned revenue would miss expectations as customers shifted spending toward AI infrastructure.
The decline weighed on broader software sentiment.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs jumps after profit beat and trading surge
bullets: Goldman Sachs shares rose about 9% during Tuesday’s market action.
Reuters reported the bank topped profit estimates on stronger trading and dealmaking.
Equities revenue surged as market volatility lifted trading activity.

articles.title: Dell rises as tech gains lead broader market higher
bullets: Dell was among the notable gainers in Tuesday’s U.S. session.
Investing.com listed Dell up 7.13% in the market close report.
Technology, basic materials and oil-and-gas shares helped lift indexes.

articles.title: Nvidia advances as AI-chip export developments stay in focus
bullets: Nvidia gained 4.06% in Tuesday’s U.S. close report.
Reuters reported that a U.S. official said very few H200 chips had begun shipping to China or Hong Kong.
The stock remained tied to investor focus on AI-chip demand and export policy.

articles.title: Biogen falls more than 8% among worst Nasdaq performers
bullets: Biogen lost 8.27% in Tuesday trading, according to Investing.com’s close report.
The decline made it one of the weakest major U.S. stocks of the session.
IBM and HCA also ranked among the day’s worst performers.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Top corporate and economic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: JPMorgan posts record quarterly profit on dealmaking and trading boom
bullets: Reuters reported JPMorgan earned $21.2 billion in the second quarter.
The result was described as the highest quarterly profit ever posted by a U.S. bank, according to LSEG data.
Investment banking fees and equity trading revenue surged.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs profit beats estimates as equities trading hits record
bullets: Goldman Sachs exceeded second-quarter profit expectations.
Reuters said Middle East war-driven volatility boosted equities trading to a record.
The bank also benefited from a stronger corporate dealmaking environment.

articles.title: Wall Street bank earnings surge, though executives warn on risks
bullets: Reuters reported major Wall Street banks posted stronger second-quarter earnings.
M&A advisory fees and trading revenue were key drivers.
Some banks still warned about economic and market risks ahead.

articles.title: CleanSpark signs $6.6 billion, 20-year data-center lease
bullets: CleanSpark agreed to a 20-year data-center infrastructure lease.
Bloomberg Law reported the deal covers its Sandersville, Georgia campus.
The lease is expected to generate $6.6 billion in contracted revenue.

articles.title: Thomson Reuters cuts engineering roles while expanding AI hiring
bullets: Reuters reported Thomson Reuters is cutting a small number of engineering jobs.
The company said it is aggressively deploying AI across its businesses.
It also expects to hire more than 250 AI-focused engineering roles over two years.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: New York imposes one-year pause on large new data centers
bullets: Reuters reported New York became the first U.S. state to halt construction of large new data centers.
The one-year moratorium targets concerns over power costs, water use and local burdens.
The move comes as AI demand drives rapid data-center expansion.

articles.title: Meta reins in Muse Image after Instagram privacy backlash
bullets: AP reported Meta pulled back a new AI feature tied to public Instagram images.
The decision followed criticism over how public profile images could be referenced.
Meta said it intended to provide creative tools while giving users control.

articles.title: Nvidia H200 shipments to China begin, but U.S. official says volumes are very small
bullets: Reuters reported a top U.S. official told Congress that very few H200 chips had shipped to China or Hong Kong.
The H200 is one of Nvidia’s most powerful AI processors.
The shipments come amid continuing U.S.–China export-control scrutiny.

articles.title: IBM warns AI infrastructure spending is squeezing software budgets
bullets: Reuters reported IBM forecast second-quarter revenue below estimates.
The company said businesses were favoring data-center infrastructure over software spending.
The warning triggered a broader software-sector selloff.

articles.title: U.S. Commerce official says AI and semiconductor regulation is coming
bullets: Reuters reported a U.S. Commerce official said new action on AI and chips is forthcoming.
The official said the Trump administration does not plan to replace the prior AI diffusion rule directly.
Chip export policy remains central to U.S. technology strategy.
